paper_title: Study of Sediments from IODP Site U1314

methods.description: Process for analyzing sediment cores from IODP Site U1314 to determine sedimentation rates and environmental conditions using isotopic and elemental measurements.

steps.description: Records were made using sediments from IODP Site U1314 (56.36° N, 27.88° W, 2820 m depth) from the southern Gardar Drift in the subpolar North Atlantic.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from IODP Site U1314

steps.description: Sedimentation rates average 9.3 cm ka−1 from 1069 to 779 ka, dated by tuning our benthic δ18O curve to the benthic isotope stack of Lisiecki and Raymo (2005, LR04) using AnalySeries software.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate sedimentation rates using benthic δ18O curve and LR04 stack

steps.description: Subsurface water column conditions were determined through Mg/Ca ratios and stable isotopes measured on deep-dwelling planktonic foraminifera N. pachyderma (sin.).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze Mg/Ca ratios and stable isotopes on N. pachyderma (sin.)

steps.description: Around 50-60 well-preserved tests of planktonic foraminifera N. pachyderma (sin.) were analyzed in 542 samples for Mg/Ca ratio following the procedure of Pena et al. (2005) which includes a reductive cleaning step.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Mg/Ca ratio analysis on N. pachyderma (sin.) samples

steps.description: Dissolved samples were analyzed using a Perkin Elmer Elan 6000 inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometer (ICP-MS) at the Scientific and Technological Centers of the University of Barcelona (CCiT-UB).
Stable isotopes (carbon and oxygen) records from benthic and planktonic foraminifera were analyzed and adjusted for uniformity.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze and adjust stable isotope records for uniformity

steps.description: Oxygen isotope values were ice-volume corrected by scaling to the sea-level curve of LR04 using a last glacial maximum to late Holocene sea-level change of 120 m.
steps.searchable_summary: correct oxygen isotope values using LR04 sea-level curve
